chapter.title: Introduction to SaxonForms
chapter.para: SaxonForms implements XForms behavior using Interactive XSLT 3.0 and SaxonJS. This guide maps the XForms 1.1 specification to concrete runtime structures in the repository so developers can understand behavior, locate implementation points, and validate conformance incrementally.

section.title: Overview and scope
section.para: SaxonForms is an XSLT 3.0 implementation of the W3C XForms 1.1 specification that runs entirely in the browser via the SaxonJS interactive XSLT engine. Rather than implementing XForms as a native browser plugin or a JavaScript framework, SaxonForms leverages XSLT template matching and function evaluation to process XForms markup, maintain model state, and render HTML controls — all within the SaxonJS runtime.

The implementation consists of approximately 9,700 lines of XSLT across five source files, compiled into a single SEF (Stylesheet Export File) that SaxonJS loads at runtime. The main transform (saxon-xforms.xsl, ~8,100 lines) contains the model lifecycle, control rendering, action dispatch, and submission logic. Supporting packages provide the XForms function library, XPath function implementations, XSD type validation helpers, and an optional logger.

Historical context and a walkthrough of the SaxonForms approach are available in the conference presentation at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GWvl7EhsocI. This guide does not rehash the XForms specification but provides a clear mapping between specification sections and the implementation, with UML diagrams illustrating structural and behavioral aspects.

section.title: Guide structure
section.para: The guide follows the XForms 1.1 specification structure:

listitem.para: SaxonForms and SaxonJS — Subsystem boundaries, component architecture, and package map.
Processing Model (XForms Ch. 4) — Lifecycle events, deferred update cycle.
Data Types and MIPs (XForms Ch. 5–6) — XSD support, model item properties, binding resolution.
XPath and Controls (XForms Ch. 7–9) — Function rewriting, control rendering, custom controls.
Actions and Submission (XForms Ch. 10–11) — Action dispatch, submission transport.
Conformance — W3C test suite status, implementation-defined behavior.
Additional Topics — Reusable components, accessibility, BPMN workflows, XSLT API reference.

section.title: Primary use-cases
section.para: The use-case diagram enumerates the implementation-facing scenarios that the rest of the guide traces back to source templates, functions, and Playwright assertions. Four actor roles interact with SaxonForms:

listitem.para: Form Author — Writes XForms markup using standard controls and XPath bindings. Relies on correct rendering and function evaluation.
Application Developer — Integrates SaxonForms into web applications, using actions (insert, delete, toggle, script, load), submission, and file upload.
End User — Interacts with rendered forms, triggering lifecycle events and control updates through the DOM event bridge.
QA Engineer — Validates conformance using the W3C Playwright test suite (tests/w3c/ch02–ch11, appendix) and regression gate.

section.title: Terminology and conventions
section.para: The following terms are used consistently throughout this guide:

listitem.para: The Saxonica Interactive XSLT 3.0 engine for the browser. Provides the ixsl:* extension functions for DOM access, event handling, and JavaScript interop.
Stylesheet Export File — a compiled XSLT representation that SaxonJS loads and executes. Built from saxon-xforms.xsl via xslt3 compiler.
A SaxonJS execution mode where XSLT templates respond to DOM events (e.g., ixsl:onclick, ixsl:onchange) and modify the page in place.
An XML document bound to an xforms:model that stores form data. Identified by @id and accessed via xforms:instance().
An xforms:bind element associating an XPath nodeset with model item properties (MIPs).
Model Item Property — attributes on xforms:bind that control node behavior: readonly, relevant, required, constraint, calculate, type.
The sequence recalculate → revalidate → refresh that runs after user interactions or action execution.
An in-memory map(*) built by the set-action mode template (L847) that stores parsed action metadata for runtime dispatch.

section.para: Line number references (e.g., L154, L5205) refer to src/saxon-xforms.xsl unless a different file is specified.

listitem.para: DocBook source validity and XInclude closure for this chapter.
Consistency between use-case language and component/package terms used in later chapters.
Verify terminology definitions match actual variable/template names in source.
User review required before proceeding to processing-model chapters.
